title: gagea graeca subsp. trinervia
intro: Gagea graeca subsp. trinervia, a species of plant in the lily family, is also known as the three-veined Gagea. It is a bulbous perennial that grows in the wild in the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, Gagea graeca subsp. trinervia has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system.
